title: URGENT: extend pss-document-service to render PO PDFs
description: Decision 2026-07-03: PO PDF generation moves into pss-document-service rather than a WeasyPrint sidecar or Node-side rendering. Port the legacy templates (app/templates/po_pdf.html + partials/certs_table.html, app/static/css/pdf_style.css, certs_table.json, base64 logo) into a doc-service endpoint the new app calls server-side. Output must stay visually identical to legacy WeasyPrint PDFs (paged-media page-X-of-Y footers). CROSS-REPO: the doc-service change is made in unison with the document-service chat/session — this bead tracks the purchase-order side and the coordination.
notes:
HANDOVER BRIEF for the document-service chat (prepared 2026-07-04, stage 2a complete):
- Port from legacy C:/Dev/PSS/purchase_order: app/templates/po_pdf.html, app/templates/partials/certs_table.html, app/static/css/pdf_style.css (WeasyPrint paged-media, page X of Y footers), app/data/certs_table.json, logo app/static/img/PSS_Standard_RGB.png (embedded base64 in legacy).
- Visual parity REQUIRED with legacy WeasyPrint output (POs go to suppliers).
- Data shape: the new app can send either po_id (doc service queries Supabase itself) or a fully-composed payload (header+metadata+line items+supplier+delivery+totals). Prefer composed payload: keeps doc-service stateless re: PO schema. Totals: net = sum(qty*unit_price) over active items, vat = net*0.2 (hardcoded in legacy), gross = net*1.2.
- Filename convention: <po_number %06d>-<revision>.pdf (e.g. 007062-1.pdf).
- Auth: per-app DOC_SERVICE_API_KEY pattern (see pss-production-card/.env.example).
- OPEN DESIGN QUESTIONS to settle in that session: (1) legacy prints the certs table on EVERY PDF, ignoring test_certificates_required — replicate or gate on the flag? (2) archive step: legacy wrote atomically to NETWORK_ARCHIVE_DIR (/mnt/share/Purchase Orders SMB on the Pi) — does archiving move into doc-service (it already stores docs) or stay an app-side write? (3) does view-pdf regenerate on miss like legacy or always serve the stored artifact?
DESIGN AGREED with Steve (2026-07-05 session):
(1) Filing = 'ISO as plumbing': POs file through the existing /api/file flow, ISO doc number is internal only; po_number/revision/project in document metadata; human-facing names stay PO-based (email attachment 'PO 007062 rev 1.pdf'). Check registry searchability by metadata in the doc-service session.
(2) PDF generation = CALLER-SUPPLIED HTML render: new doc-service endpoint takes HTML + filing metadata, renders via pooled headless Chromium, mints doc number, stores, returns {id, doc_number, url}. Input-agnostic for all PSS apps; PO app composes HTML from its own preview markup.
(3) Engineering notes for the doc-service session: pooled browser + render timeout; page-X-of-Y via print footer templates OWNED BY THE SERVICE (consistent furniture across apps); block external resource fetches during render (inline/data-URI assets only — SSRF + determinism); API-key gated.
(4) Consequence accepted by Steve: this kills the legacy issue path's equivalence (SMB archive) — after cutover, ISSUING happens only from the new app; legacy fallback role shrinks to data entry/reading.
(5) Dev containment: run doc-service locally against the CLONE (SUPABASE_URL/SECRET via env, HOST_DOC_ROOT to a scratch dir, dev API key minted on the local instance). Live doc-service untouched until its own deliberate deployment.
ARCHITECTURE REVISED (Steve, evening session): PDF rendering moves OUT of doc-service into a NEW standalone pss-pdf-service (HTML->PDF only, pooled Chromium, no DB). Doc-service gains ONE additive route /api/file-html (html + filing metadata -> internally calls pdf-service -> existing filing pipeline -> {id, doc_number, url}); all other doc-service code untouched. App flow: preview PDFs call pdf-service directly (ephemeral); issue calls doc-service /api/file-html; stamp issued_doc_id/issued_doc_number on the PO row (additive columns, cutover-time). Legacy consumers of doc-service pdfkit reports migrate to pdf-service gradually/never. FINAL DESIGN CALLS: certs table ALWAYS included (legacy parity); NO draft banner; ISO number internal-only, email attachment named 'PO NNNNNN rev R.pdf'. Repo ukstevem/pss-pdf-service created by Steve; scaffold + build tonight (no VPN needed).
PO PDF LAYOUT APPROVED by Steve (v6, evening 2026-07-04/05) — reference frozen at docs/po-pdf-template/approved-sample-v6.html + sample-generator.py. Layout decisions: logo cropped to artwork bbox (PNG has ~19% transparent padding) rendered 92px top-aligned with header text; global box-sizing:border-box (right-border clip fix); totals rows in tbody so they print once (thead column headers DO repeat per page); standards/certs table always on its own final page, TOP-aligned, legacy blue styling; service footers left='PO NNNNNN • Rev R', centre page X of Y, right printed date. Print component (PoPrintDocument) to be built from this reference against clone data.
Doc-service side SHIPPED (their session): POST /api/file-html live on main (bb60abd/57cd601), contract recorded — html<=2MB, footer/page passthrough, iso_description_id (53=ORDER/CD), project_number, original_file_name -> 201 {id, doc_number, url,...}; RENDER_FAILED 502 files nothing (render-before-mint). No registry metadata search — issued_doc_id/issued_doc_number stamp on PO row is the contract, original_file_name ilike as forensics. Their acceptance vs clone staged, blocked on VPN. App-side integration tracked in new bead.
close_reason: COMPLETE across all three repos: pss-pdf-service built+verified (HTML->PDF, pooled Chromium); doc-service /api/file-html shipped with tg7 mint-before-render + void rows (constraint fix 22z applied to clone); PO app composes frozen v6 template, previews via pdf-service (watermarked), files at issue via doc-service, stamps issued_doc_id/issued_doc_number. Full acceptance passed + Steve's e2e click tests. Remaining PDF-adjacent work lives in: 9bq.7 (Outlook draft — now unblocked), 9bq.29 (deploy incl. pdf-service to Orin + doc-service live deploy with void migration), 9bq.32 (post-cutover backfill).

title: Issue flow: PDF preview + file-html filing integration
description: Build the app side of the agreed PDF contract (doc-service /api/file-html shipped, see 9bq.2 notes): (1) PoPrintDocument HTML composer from the frozen v6 template (cropped logo data-URI, certs table always, net/VAT20/grand); (2) GET /po/[id]/pdf route streaming an ephemeral preview via pss-pdf-service /render; (3) on transition to issued, call doc-service /api/file-html (iso_description_id=53 ORDER, original_file_name 'PO NNNNNN rev R.pdf') and stamp issued_doc_id/issued_doc_number on the PO row (additive columns, db/po_doc_columns.sql — apply to clone when VPN returns); (4) File PDF retry button on preview when issued+unfiled; double-issue guard = skip filing when issued_doc_id already set (doc-service dedup will not fire on rendered bytes). RISK flagged: contract says project_number is 5 digits — our projects include 4-char '0005'/'0006'; verify the filing rule accepts them during acceptance.
notes:
App side BUILT against the shipped contract (tsc + next build green; commit pushed). Cannot runtime-verify tonight — clone VPN down. Staged for VPN return, in order: (1) psql clone -f db/po_doc_columns.sql; (2) doc-service session runs C:/tmp/file-html-acceptance.sh (registers app key + filing rule — grab the minted DOC_SERVICE_API_KEY from it); (3) set DOC_SERVICE_URL/KEY in local/.env, rebuild container; (4) e2e: Preview PDF on a real PO, issue a test PO -> auto-file -> Filed badge, stop pdf-service -> retry button path. RISKS to verify: project_number '0005' (4-char) vs 5-digit contract; local doc-service instance port for DOC_SERVICE_URL.
RISK RESOLVED (Steve): doc-service rejects 4-digit references — project_number is now left-padded to 5 digits in the client ('0005' files as '00005'); PO-side data keeps the unpadded identity. Watermark finalised at 20% opacity, preview-only.
Steve approved doc-service tg7 (mint-before-render): filed PO PDFs will carry the ISO doc number in the service footer — self-identifying on paper. Render failure still files nothing but MAY burn a serial (accepted). Expect the doc number in the footer during tomorrow's e2e; previews remain number-free + watermarked (extra visual distinction from the filed artefact).
E2E VERIFIED (2026-07-05, VPN back): columns applied to clone; doc-service acceptance ALL PASSED after fixing their void-row constraint bug (pss-document-service-22z, migration applied to clone + pushed); PO app rebuilt with DOC_SERVICE_URL (local :8090 instance) + minted key; preview route serves real clone PO 007062-0005 through composer->pdf-service in 0.68s, watermark on every page. Remaining: Steve's click test — File PDF retry on issued 7062 (stamp + badge) and a fresh create->issue auto-file.
Steve decisions: (1) SMB share RETIRED for the new system — registry is the archive; legacy keeps writing its own copies while it lives, share stops growing at cutover. (2) UX gap found in step-2 test: no visible way to issue an approved PO — adding an Issue PO button on the preview (draft/approved) that runs the status transition + auto-file, instead of requiring the edit form's status dropdown.
close_reason: Steve verified both paths on the clone stack: (1) File PDF retry on issued 7062 -> stamped 00005-A&CD-0001, filed artifact linked from badge, footer carries ISO number, padding 0005->00005 confirmed; (2) create -> approve -> Issue PO button -> auto-file with ISO number in footer. Watermarked previews distinct from filed artifacts. SMB share retired (registry is the archive); backfill of legacy PDFs is post-cutover 9bq.32 (approach A).

title: Create/edit PO form UI fixes (Steve's polish list)
description: UI polish pass on PoForm from Steve's stage-2a review, 2026-07-04. Item 1: project/item, supplier, delivery address, delivery contact as full-width fields stacked one above the other (was 2x2 grid). Further items appended as notes as Steve steps through.
notes:
Item 1 done: project/item, supplier, address, contact now full-width stacked (SearchSelect gained a className prop; dropdown follows input width). Deployed to local container.
Item 2 done: status + bump-revision selects moved to the form footer, left of the Save button; Save right-aligned. Current status/rev caption sits beside them. Deployed to local container.
Item 3 done: project/item combo ordered 0005, 0006 sticky then projects descending (item_seq ascending within each) — applied in fetchProjectItemOptions via shared orderProjectItemRows helper. Verified in payload: 0005, 0006, 10359, 10358...
close_reason: All three items done and Steve-approved: (1) full-width stacked pickers, (2) status/bump at footer left of right-aligned Save, (3) project/item combo sticky 0005/0006 then descending. Deployed to local container.

title: Deploy to Pi: ARM64 image, compose stack, gateway rebuild
description: Production deployment, parked by Steve 2026-07-04 ('not yet') pending stage-2 completion: (1) ./build.sh — ARM64 image to ghcr.io/ukstevem/pss-purchase-order (needs docker buildx + ghcr auth); (2) on the Pi: clone repo to /opt/pss-purchase-order, docker compose -f docker-compose.app.yml up -d (PO_WRITES_ENABLED unset = read-only); (3) rebuild + redeploy gateway image so production.conf 3017 block goes live (platform-portal commit 9346bed already pushed); (4) apply db/po_write_functions.sql to LIVE Supabase at write-cutover only, along with secrets rotation (gcc.2) and the dual-write single-writer convention (grill-me Q4).
notes:
CUTOVER PLANNED for tomorrow (2026-07-06, Steve in office): full runbook at PSS-Vault/projects/pss-purchase-order/cutover-runbook.md — pdf-service to Orin (needs prod compose artifacts first), doc-service deploy + void migration + live app registration, LIVE Supabase DDL (3 files in db/), PO app to Pi, gateway rebuild, read-only smoke, arm writes, live test, then cutover + Steve restricts legacy access. 9bq.7 closes first with the clone auto-cycle test.
Step 1 DONE: pss-pdf-service live on Orin via Portainer repo-stack (image fa05ba5). Verified from dev machine: healthz ok, 330ms render with prod key, 401 on bad key. Prod PDF_SERVICE_API_KEY canonical in platform-portal/.env.
close_reason: CUTOVER COMPLETE 2026-07-06: pss-pdf-service live on Orin :8017; doc-service redeployed with /api/file-html + void migration + PDF env passthrough (compose fix pushed); purchase-order app registered on live registry; live Supabase DDL applied (write functions, doc columns, email column); PO app on Pi :3017 via gateway /purchase-order/ with Azure login; writes armed. Live-verified: read-only smoke (1,225 POs), watermarked preview via Orin, full issue cycle — PO 007257 rev 2 issued, filed 00005-A&CD-0001 (first PO in the registry, KEPT as real), drafted to purchasing@; 7258 created+cancelled as transition test. Fixes en route: Pi DNS AAAA stalls (8s->0.19s), crypto.randomUUID on HTTP origin, same-revision transitions in-place (legacy latent bug gcc.11). Steve restricting legacy access.

title: Stage 2a: legacy-against-clone oracle container
description: Acceptance harness: run legacy image locally on port 5051 against the clone via docker run env injection (SUPABASE_URL/SECRET_KEY/SUPABASE_KEY overridden; EMAIL_DRAFT_ON_PO=0 and MS_* neutered so no Outlook drafts; archive dir internal). Zero changes to the legacy repo. Acceptance: bidirectional round-trips - new-app PO renders in legacy UI, legacy edits render in new app, revision walk a->b->c with single active snapshot.
close_reason: Oracle live: po-legacy-oracle container (image built from unmodified legacy source, .env excluded by dockerignore) on http://localhost:5051 against the clone via env injection; EMAIL_DRAFT_ON_PO=0, no MS_* creds. po-list renders 1000 clone POs.

title: Stage 2a: create_po_revision RPC + create/edit PO forms
description: Third write slice: atomic Postgres function create_po_revision (insert new purchase_orders row + metadata + line items, deactivate old snapshot, revision bump a,b,c->1,2,3, last_release stamping) applied to CLONE only; create + edit forms per legacy po_form.html (dynamic line items, project/item dropdowns, supplier, structured delivery only - manual address stays disabled); stale-revision check before save; status transitions forward-only + cancel (status_utils parity).
notes: Implemented and deployed to local container. SQL functions lifecycle-tested on clone (test PO 007062-0005: create a -> draft edit b -> stale rejection -> release rev 1 + last_release -> in-place issued edit). Create page renders 343 project/item options + supplier type-ahead; edit page loads full initial state. Found+fixed: delivery_contacts has no organisation column (spec vs schema drift); po_metadata unique embed returned as object not array (also silently blanked preview metadata since phase 1). Awaiting Steve UI round-trip vs oracle.
close_reason: Steve confirmed the full create/edit click-path incl. item 6 two-tab stale rejection (after the superseded-snapshot detection fix). Revision walk, in-place vs bump, oracle cross-checks all verified against the clone.

title: Stage 2a: accounts writes (acc_complete, invoice_reference)
description: Second write slice: checkbox immediate save, invoice ref debounced 500ms + blur save, PATCH purchase_orders (legacy accounts.py parity), fail closed, gated by PO_WRITES_ENABLED.
notes: Implemented + deployed to local container: AccountsTable with checkbox immediate save, invoice ref debounced 500ms + blur (legacy accounts.html semantics), updatePoAccounts server action PATCHing purchase_orders (gated by PO_WRITES_ENABLED, fail-closed banner). Verified writable=true reaching UI, 1027 rows, no errors. Awaiting Steve round-trip vs oracle.
close_reason: Steve round-trip tested accounts writes vs the oracle during the create/edit build; confirmed working in the full stage-2a test pass 2026-07-04.

title: Stage 2a: write plumbing (PO_WRITES_ENABLED) + expediting writes
description: First write slice per grill-me plan: PO_WRITES_ENABLED runtime flag read server-side and passed to UI; server action updateLineItem with field whitelist {qty_received, exped_expected_date, exped_completed_date}, clamp 0..qty, auto-stamp/clear exped_completed_date on full receipt (legacy expediting.html JS parity), fail-closed error surfacing; expediting expansion rows become editable when flag on. Dev target: clone (local/.env).
notes: Implemented + deployed to local container (writes armed via local/.env PO_WRITES_ENABLED=1). Verified: writable flag reaches UI, clone accepts the update shape (probe PATCH 200 on real line item). Remaining: Steve's click-through — edit received/dates on expediting, confirm auto-stamp behaviour, cross-check same PO in oracle at :5051.
close_reason: Steve confirmed bidirectional round-trip: expediting edits in new app visible in legacy oracle and vice versa; auto-stamp behaviour verified. Amended per Steve: over-receipt now ALLOWED in new system (floor 0 only, server + client; legacy keeps its clamp) — surfacing tracked in 9bq.28. Container rebuilt with the change.

title: Stage-2 dev env: local Supabase clone (10.0.0.85) + password-auth fallback
description: Steve 2026-07-04: stage-2 (write) development must run against the self-hosted Supabase clone at http://10.0.0.85:8000 to protect live data; creds provided in git-ignored local/.env (var names match app exactly). Work: (1) document/wire docker-compose.local.yml --env-file ./local/.env mode; (2) port orderbook's SignInPanel pattern — NEXT_PUBLIC_ALLOW_PASSWORD_AUTH=1 enables email/password sign-in for the clone (Azure OAuth won't be configured there); flag is build-time and never set by build.sh so production keeps Azure-only. STATUS: host currently unreachable from dev machine (100% ping loss, nothing on 8000/5432) — Steve checking. Verify on reconnect: PO schema tables present in clone, auth user exists in clone auth.users, AuthProvider's auth_users/jobcard_is_superuser dependencies exist in clone.
notes: VM back up. Verified clone at 10.0.0.85:8000: full PO schema (purchase_orders 2106, po_line_items 9363, suppliers 411, project_register 270, views present) — snapshot slightly behind live (1030 active vs 1225). Container rebuilt in clone mode and serving clone data. auth_users view + jobcard_is_superuser RPC present. Dev user dev@pss.local created via admin API (password given to Steve in chat). Remaining: Steve confirms password sign-in in browser.
close_reason: Steve signed in to the clone via dev@pss.local password auth; container running against 10.0.0.85 clone. Stage-2 dev environment complete.

title: Expediting: due-in-24h and due-this-work-week tables
description: Test finding 2026-07-04 (Steve, expediting item 4). Small tables above the expediting list: outstanding line items (active, no completed date, not fully received, PO approved/issued) due (a) today/tomorrow and (b) the rest of the work week through Friday (disjoint windows, Europe/London; on weekends the week table covers Mon-Fri ahead). Columns: PO, project, supplier, description, qty, received, expected. Steve floated possibly a separate page — starting on-page, revisit if noisy.
close_reason: Due tables live above the expediting list: 'Due within 24 hours' (today+tomorrow) and 'Due this work week (to 10/07)' — Sat correctly targets coming Friday. Outstanding lines only (no completed date, not fully received, PO approved/issued), PO links to preview, empty state 'Nothing due.'

title: Expediting: PO-list filters + server pagination + delivery filter
description: Test finding 2026-07-04 (Steve, expediting items 1-2). Same filter bar as po-list (status, project ordered w/ sticky, supplier type-ahead, date range) and same pagination rules (50/page server-side, filters persist, page clamps). Plus a delivery filter (Late / In progress / Complete). Delivery status is derived from line items, so that filter path fetches all matching POs + line items (chunked in() queries), computes flags, then paginates in memory; without it the fast count+range path is used.
close_reason: Verified in container: expediting has po-list filter bar (status/project sticky-ordered/supplier type-ahead/dates) + delivery filter (Late/In progress/Complete), server pagination 50/page 'Showing 1-50 of 1,225', delivery=late computes flags across all matching POs (552 late) with chunked line-item fetches, page links carry delivery param, combined filters 200.

title: PO list: server-side pagination, 50/page, filters persist
description: Test finding 2026-07-04 (Steve, item 6). Paginate the PO list at 50 rows/page with filters (status/project/supplier/dates/sort) persisting across pages; changing a filter resets to page 1. Implemented server-side via PostgREST range + exact count (count query first, page clamped, then row fetch) — which also removes the 1000-row truncation flagged in 9bq.10.
close_reason: Server-side pagination verified in container: 50 rows/page, 'Showing 1-50 of 1,225' exact count, page 2 with status=issued keeps filter (all rows issued, next link carries status), page=99999 clamps to 25 of 25. Filter change resets to page 1 via FilterBar.

title: Type-ahead too restrictive: punctuation blocks matches
description: Follow-up to 9bq.12 (Steve, 2026-07-04): 'c rob' fails to find 'C. Roberts Steel Services Ltd.' because the dot breaks the literal substring. Switch SearchSelect to token matching over normalised text (lowercase, punctuation to spaces): every typed token must appear in the name, so 'c rob', 'c. rob', 'rob steel' all match.
close_reason: Token matching over normalised text deployed to local container. Matcher cases verified: 'c rob', 'c. rob', 'rob steel', 'ROBERTS LTD', double-spaces and padded input all match 'C. Roberts Steel Services Ltd.'; garbage doesn't.

title: Supplier filter: type-ahead with substring matching
description: Test finding 2026-07-04 (Steve, item 4). Plain <select> only jumps by first letter, but suppliers are known by mid-name terms (e.g. 'C Roberts' is looked up as 'roberts') — a common lookup problem. Replace with a searchable combobox: typing filters the option list by case-insensitive substring anywhere in the name; picking an option applies the exact supplier_name filter (server semantics unchanged); Enter selects first match; x clears. Applied on po-list and accounts supplier dropdowns via new SearchSelect component behind a searchable flag on FilterBar.
close_reason: SearchSelect type-ahead live on po-list and accounts supplier filters, verified wired in container (searchable prop present, supplier list includes 'C. Roberts Steel Services Ltd.' which 'rob' substring-matches). Server filter semantics unchanged (exact eq on pick).

title: Project filter dropdowns: sort high-to-low with 0005/0006 sticky
description: Test finding 2026-07-04 (Steve, item 3). PO-list project filter dropdown must sort descending (highest project first) with 0005 and 0006 pinned at the top — same ordering rule as the dashboard (9bq.8). Applied via shared orderProjectOptions() helper; accounts page project dropdown gets the same treatment for consistency.
close_reason: Verified in container: po-list project dropdown now 0005, 0006, then 10380 descending. Shared orderProjectOptions()/STICKY_PROJECTS in lib/po-logic.ts; accounts project dropdown and dashboard use the same source.

title: Dashboard counts wrong: active_po_list aggregation capped at 1000 rows
description: Test finding 2026-07-04 (Steve, item 2). Dashboard draft/active counts are wrong — some projects 0, others short (e.g. 10344 shows 4 active vs 5 in the drill-down list). Root cause: fetchProjectPoSummary selects active_po_list without pagination, so PostgREST's 1000-row default cap truncates the aggregation (same class of bug as legacy purchase_order-gr3 on accounts; legacy dashboard had it too). Fix: offset-loop the fetch like fetchAccountsOverview. Secondary: drill-down list includes drafts while 'Active' excludes them — verify 10344 splits 4 active + 1 draft after fix.
close_reason: Fixed and verified in container: fetchProjectPoSummary now offset-loops active_po_list past the 1000-row PostgREST cap (ordered by po_number for stable paging). 10344 verified: dashboard 0 draft / 5 active == drill-down 5 issued rows, exact project_id matches.

title: Dashboard: source projects from project_register, paginate, sticky 0005/0006
description: Test finding 2026-07-04 (Steve, item 1). Dashboard 'Purchase Orders by Project': (1) project list must come from project_register (canonical) instead of being derived from active_po_list project_id values, which surface deprecated project references; (2) paginate the table; (3) sort projects descending (highest project number first) EXCEPT 0005 and 0006 which are pinned to the top (sticky). PO counts still aggregate from active_po_list per legacy draft/non-draft rule. Note: projects appearing on POs but absent from project_register will no longer show — flagged to Steve.
close_reason: Dashboard reworked and verified in local container: projects sourced from project_register (register shows through 10380 vs 10168 max previously — deprecated-source symptom gone), 0005+0006 pinned top with amber tint, remainder descending, paginated 50/page (Page 1 of 6, ?page=2 link verified). Totals row covers all projects.

title: Phase 1 — read-only parity
description: Decision 2026-07-03: new app starts READ-ONLY against the shared production Supabase. Pages: dashboard (draft/active counts), PO list w/ filters (active_po_list view), PO web preview, expediting view (no editing), accounts view (no updates), spend report. Server-side data access via lazy getSupabaseAdmin() (SUPABASE_URL + SUPABASE_SECRET_KEY); all pages gated by Azure login (Supabase OAuth via vendored @platform/auth). Verify rendered data side-by-side against legacy UI.
notes:
All six read-only pages implemented and runtime-verified against live Supabase via dev server on 3017: dashboard (project summary + drill-down), po-list (~1000-row cap parity, status/project/supplier/date filters server-side), PO preview (007256-0006 format, sorted line items, accounting totals), expediting (50/page in-memory pagination, delivery flags, batched line-item fetch), accounts (offset-loop full fetch, completed/project/supplier filters, read-only inputs), spend report (12 months ending Jul 2026, drill-down links). Probes: bad uuid -> graceful alert; ?status=draft -> 11/11 draft; page=9999 clamps. Remaining before close: Steve's browser check (Azure login + visual parity vs legacy), ARM64 image build, Pi deploy, gateway registration commit.
Local test container running: docker-compose.local.yml (amd64 build on dev machine, purchase-order-local, healthy). Verified serving live data at http://localhost:3017/purchase-order/. Awaiting Steve's browser test incl. Azure login.
close_reason: Read-only parity complete and superseded by verified stage-2a work: all six pages tested by Steve against live (2026-07-04 session) incl. Azure login on localhost; runtime-verified via RSC payloads. Deployment steps moved to pss-purchase-order-9bq.29.

notes: PORTS.md 3017 row + 3018 reserved, production.conf redirect + proxy block added in platform-portal working tree. Copy conventions from pss-production-card (compose/build) + pss-matl-cert (app internals).

title: Scaffold repo: git init, gitignore, env example, beads, README
description: Bootstrap pss-purchase-order per pss-standalone-app skill: git init -b main + origin github.com/ukstevem/pss-purchase-order, .gitignore (secrets excluded), .env.example, NEW_STANDALONE_APP.md copied from platform-portal docs, bd init, README with identity block (port 3017, /purchase-order/, ghcr.io/ukstevem/pss-purchase-order).

title: Rotate exposed secrets from legacy .env
description: Legacy .env (git-ignored but on disk) holds a live Supabase service-role key (SECRET_KEY, sb_secret_...), publishable key, and MS Graph client secret. SECRET_KEY is dual-purposed as both Flask session-signing secret and Supabase service-role key (app/__init__.py:22 vs supabase_client.py:20). Rotate all of these when the new system takes over; new app sources secrets from canonical platform-portal/.env. Do NOT rotate while legacy is live without updating its .env at the same moment.

title: Column-name mismatch: po_preview reads qty_recevied / exped_ccompleted_date
description: routes.py:249-250 (po_preview) reads misspelled columns qty_recevied and exped_ccompleted_date, while expediting.py:139-141,186 reads qty_received / exped_completed_date. Only one spelling can match the real Supabase schema, so either the web-preview expediting status or the expediting page is silently broken. VERIFY actual column names in Supabase before porting (live-DB query was not authorized during the survey). New app must use the confirmed spelling everywhere.
notes: VERIFIED during phase-1 runtime testing (2026-07-03): live po_line_items returns qty_received / exped_completed_date with real values — the correctly-spelled columns ARE the schema. Therefore legacy po_preview (routes.py:249-250) reading qty_recevied/exped_ccompleted_date is the silently-broken side: its delivered/partial statuses never fire from receipt data. New app uses correct names throughout (lib/po-logic.ts).
close_reason: Resolved by verification 2026-07-04: live schema confirmed as qty_received / exped_completed_date (expediting queries + writes work against them). Legacy po_preview's misspelled reads are the broken side — per policy the legacy repo stays untouched; the new app uses correct names everywhere and its preview expediting highlighting actually works.

title: Migrate purchase order system to standalone Next.js app
description: Port github.com/ukstevem/purchase_order (Flask, C:/Dev/PSS/purchase_order) to the PSS standalone-app pattern: Next.js behind the platform-portal gateway, port 3017, route /purchase-order/, Azure login via Supabase OAuth. Legacy system stays live and UNMODIFIED; both run side by side on the same Supabase instance until proven. Feature parity target: dashboard, PO list, create/edit with revision snapshots, PDF generation + archive, Outlook draft on issue, expediting, accounts, spend report.
close_reason: EPIC COMPLETE: legacy Flask purchase order system fully replaced by the standalone Next.js app — read parity, atomic writes, PDF registry filing with ISO numbering, Outlook drafting — deployed to production and cut over 2026-07-06. Remaining work tracked in open child/related beads (secrets rotation, backfill, RLS, improvements).

title: Flag over-received line items (received > ordered qty)
description: Follow-up to the over-receipt change (Steve, 2026-07-04): the new system deliberately allows qty_received > quantity (legacy clamps; legacy unchanged). Over-receipts need surfacing 'somehow' later — candidates: amber over-received badge on the expediting line/PO flag, a filterable delivery state, inclusion in the supplier OTD/performance reporting (9bq.18), or an accounts-side check before invoice matching. Decide presentation with Steve when picked up.

title: Role-based write permissions (with RLS phase)
description: Grill-me decision 2026-07-04 (Q3): stage 2 allows any authenticated (Azure) user to write — parity-plus. Proper roles (e.g. purchasing group can create/edit/issue; others read-only) to be built later, enforced at the database level as part of the RLS conversion (pss-purchase-order-hra), not as a cosmetic UI check. Ecosystem primitives available: useRole, jobcard_project_members, jobcard_is_superuser.

title: Expediting expansion: centre qty/received/expected/completed, even widths
description: Test finding 2026-07-04 (Steve, expediting item 3). In the expanded line-item table: centre-align Qty, Received, Expected, Completed and make those columns even width (table-fixed, description 40%, 15% each).
close_reason: Expansion table now table-fixed: Description 40%, Qty/Received/Expected/Completed even at 15% each and centre-aligned. tsc clean, container rebuilt; visual check with Steve.

title: Supplier performance / OTD monitoring from expediting data
description: Test session 2026-07-04 (Steve, item 8 — flagged for later). Monitor supplier performance from expediting line-item data: on-time delivery (OTD) per supplier = exped_completed_date vs exped_expected_date, plus qty_received completeness. Candidate metrics: % lines delivered on time, late count, average days late, trend over time; aggregated per supplier (and possibly per project). Natural companion to 9bq.15 (rationalising expediting info) — consider designing the two together. Data quality caveat: expected/completed dates are only as good as expediting-page upkeep, and history predating consistent use will skew OTD; may want a cutoff date or minimum-lines threshold before scoring a supplier.
notes: Steve 2026-07-04: metric design confirmed — 3-month and 6-month rolling averages of OTD per supplier from the expediting data (or similar rolling window). Use as the starting spec when this is picked up.

title: Add/modify supplier UI (currently manual in Supabase)
description: Test session 2026-07-04 (Steve, item 7 — flagged for later, phase 2+). Suppliers are maintained by hand in Supabase today (legacy app has no supplier CRUD; seeded from Suppliers.csv/xlsx). Add an add/modify supplier form. Cautions: suppliers is dual-purpose — type is supplier/delivery/both and the same table provides delivery addresses (delivery_contacts.address_id FKs into it), so edits ripple into rendered PO delivery blocks for the LIVE legacy system sharing this table. Fields: name, address_line1, address_line2, postcode, email, type. Must sit behind the phase-2 write feature flag; consider soft-validation against near-duplicate names (the type-ahead work in 9bq.12 shows how messy lookup already is).

title: Rationalise expediting page information vs purchase orders
description: Longer-term design item from 2026-07-04 testing (Steve): the expediting page and the PO list/preview present overlapping information about the same POs (status vs delivery flags, line items in two places). Rethink how expediting data integrates with the purchase order views — possibly one consolidated view, or expediting state surfaced on the PO list/preview rather than a separate page. Needs a design discussion with Steve before any build; do not fold into phase-1 parity.

title: PO list formatting: colour-coded status pills, centre status/revision/date
description: Test finding 2026-07-04 (Steve, item 5). Colour-code the status pills (draft zinc, approved blue, issued green, complete muted, cancelled red struck-through) and centre-align the Status, Revision and Date columns on the PO list; same alignment on the expediting table for consistency. Note: legacy CSS had status-tag classes but no per-status colours defined — this is a deliberate improvement, not parity.
close_reason: Status pills colour-coded (draft zinc / approved blue / issued green / complete muted / cancelled red struck) and Status/Revision/Date centred on po-list; Delivery/Revision/Date centred on expediting. tsc clean, container rebuilt; visual confirmation with Steve (client-rendered, not curl-visible).

title: po-list still capped at 1000 rows (legacy parity) — paginate later
description: Flag-for-later from 2026-07-04 testing: the PO list fetch (fetchActivePosFromView) intentionally mirrors legacy behaviour and returns at most 1000 rows unfiltered (~1000+ POs exist, so the unfiltered list is silently truncated). Decide: offset-loop like the dashboard/accounts, or add real pagination UI like expediting. Filtered views (per-project drill-downs) are unaffected in practice.
close_reason: Superseded by 9bq.16: po-list now paginates server-side with exact count (range + count:exact), no 1000-row truncation. True total is 1,225 POs — the old cap hid 225.

title: Port MS Graph Outlook-draft flow (Issue PO)
description: Decision 2026-07-03: included in parity. Server-side MSAL client-credentials -> create draft with PO PDF attached in shared purchasing mailbox (MS_TENANT_ID/MS_CLIENT_ID/MS_CLIENT_SECRET/MS_OUTLOOK_MAILBOX). Reimplement single-flight guard FAIL-CLOSED (legacy fails open — see pss-purchase-order-gcc.7). Beware duplicate drafts while both systems live: legacy keeps EMAIL_DRAFT_ON_PO active; new app must not issue the same PO.
notes:
Built + deployed to local container (tsc/build green; email_draft_at column applied to clone). NOT yet runtime-tested against Graph: EMAIL_DRAFT_ON_PO is default-off and no MS_* creds are set in local/.env. Needs Steve: which mailbox for dev testing (real purchasing mailbox gets a deletable TEST draft, or a personal/test mailbox), then set EMAIL_DRAFT_ON_PO=1 + MS_* in local/.env and restart. Legacy creds exist in the legacy .env; drafts are never sent either way.
Draft flow VERIFIED by Steve on clone stack (draft in purchasing@, filed PDF attached, visible in desktop Outlook; webLink pop-up removed on request). Remaining before close: one full create->issue auto cycle (file+draft in one motion) — first item tomorrow.
close_reason: Steve passed the full auto-cycle on the clone stack (2026-07-06): create -> Issue PO -> filed with ISO number + purchasing@ draft with filed PDF attached, zero extra clicks. Fail-closed single-flight via email_draft_at; webLink pop-up removed; drafts reviewed in desktop Outlook. Stage 2b complete.

title: Phase 2 — writes behind feature flag
description: After phase-1 verification: create/edit PO replicating the append-only revision-snapshot model EXACTLY (deactivate old po_metadata/po_line_items, insert new purchase_orders row, same DB-generated po_number, current_revision a,b,c->1,2,3, last_release stamping), expediting inline updates, accounts updates. Gate all writes behind a feature flag so read-only mode remains the deploy default until sign-off.
notes:
Grill-me Q6 (2026-07-04): manual delivery address stays DISABLED in the new create/edit form — Steve: it was deliberately disabled in legacy because ad-hoc delivery details are provided in the line-item description instead; structured addresses (suppliers rows type delivery/both) + manual contact fields only. No other intentional behaviour changes to create/edit in stage 2 beyond the atomic RPC (Q5) and stale-revision check (Q4).
GRILL-ME PLAN AGREED (2026-07-04): (1) scope expediting->accounts->create/edit, Issue PO deferred to stage 2b behind doc-service PDF (9bq.2) + Graph (9bq.7); (2) runtime PO_WRITES_ENABLED env flag enforced in every server action, UI hides affordances when off; (3) any authed user writes, roles with RLS (9bq.23); (4) short dual-write window, single writer, legacy as fallback; stale-revision check in new app's edit flow; (5) atomic create_po_revision Postgres function (additive) replaces legacy's non-transactional 3-call snapshot - clone first, live pre-cutover; (6) create/edit exact parity, manual delivery address stays disabled (delivery info goes in line-item description); (7) expediting/accounts exact parity incl. auto-stamp/clear completed date + 0..qty clamp, saves fail closed; (8) acceptance via legacy container run against clone with env injection (no repo changes), bidirectional round-trip tests.
close_reason: Stage 2a complete: expediting, accounts, create/edit writes live behind PO_WRITES_ENABLED, atomic via db/po_write_functions.sql, bidirectionally verified against the legacy oracle on the clone. Issue-PO flow is stage 2b under 9bq.2 + 9bq.7.

title: Legacy has zero auth and wide-open CORS
description: No login, no @login_required, no user identity; CORS(app) unrestricted (app/__init__.py:32); server uses the service-role key for everything, trusting only the network boundary. The new app must gate every page and API route behind Azure login (Supabase OAuth, client-side useAuth per ecosystem pattern) and keep the service-role key server-side only. Also feeds the RLS conversion task pss-purchase-order-hra.

title: advance_revision route uses non-existent 'revision' column
description: routes.py:1184-1219 (/po/<id>/advance-revision) and its helpers _get_po/_insert_po read and write a 'revision' column; the schema everywhere else uses 'current_revision'. Route appears legacy/dead and likely 400s or writes nothing. Decide during the port whether this endpoint is needed at all — the edit-PO flow already bumps revisions.

title: Convert Supabase schema to RLS once new app proven
description: Legacy app talks to Supabase with the service-role key and no RLS; tables are wide open to any anon-key holder. After pss-purchase-order is proven in side-by-side testing, add RLS policies to purchase_orders, po_metadata, po_line_items, suppliers, delivery_contacts, project_register(_items) and move the new app to authenticated access patterns. Blocked until parity + sign-off — the legacy app must keep working unmodified until cutover.

title: Legacy purchase_order findings — log only; fix after parity
description: Defects and code smells found in the legacy Flask system during the 2026-07-03 survey. Per agreed approach: DO NOT fix in the legacy repo and do not chase these during the initial port — replicate behaviour first, improve after the new system is running. Full survey: PSS-Vault/projects/pss-purchase-order/legacy-system-survey.md

title: Legacy cannot cancel/complete a released PO (constraint violation) — log only
description: Found during live cutover testing 2026-07-06: legacy edit branch C writes a NEW snapshot with the SAME revision for issued->cancelled/complete transitions, which ux_po_number_revision always rejects — the transition was impossible in legacy (error flashed, swallowed). New app now routes same-revision transitions through the in-place update instead (deliberate fix, not parity). DO NOT fix in legacy.

title: Backfill legacy PO PDFs into the document registry
description: Steve 2026-07-05: with the SMB share retired, historical PO PDFs must come into the registry so it is the complete archive. Two approaches, decision pending: (A) INGEST ORIGINALS — walk the share's 'Purchase Orders' folder (NNNNNN-rev.pdf naming parses to po_number+revision), file each via the existing multipart /api/file (preserves the exact bytes suppliers received — audit-faithful), stamp issued_doc_id/issued_doc_number on the matching purchase_orders revision row; regenerate via file-html only for issued POs missing a share PDF. (B) RE-RENDER ALL — push every issued/complete PO through the new v6 compose->file-html pipeline (uniform template, but the artifacts are NOT the documents actually sent — ahistorical). Recommend A with B as gap-filler. Run at cutover against LIVE (needs share access + live registry); dry-run on clone first. Cautions: dedup will reject byte-identical duplicates on re-runs (good); serials will consume a large range; original_file_name should carry the legacy NNNNNN-rev.pdf name.
notes: DECIDED (Steve): approach A — ingest the original share PDFs (audit-faithful), re-render only for gaps. Scheduled POST-CUTOVER; do not build during stage 2. P3 to reflect scheduling.

title: Legacy dashboard has the same 1000-row truncation (log only)
description: Found while fixing 9bq.9 (2026-07-04): legacy fetch_project_po_summary (supabase_client.py:979) also selects active_po_list with no pagination, so the legacy dashboard's draft/active counts silently truncate at 1000 rows — same class as their closed accounts bug purchase_order-gr3. DO NOT fix in legacy repo; superseded by the new app.

title: Fragile CWD-relative asset paths in PDF generation
description: Logo and pdf_style.css are loaded as 'app/static/...' relative to the process CWD (routes.py:909,933,1024,1045) — only works because gunicorn runs from /app. In the new app resolve assets robustly (import-relative/bundled), and note the PDF pipeline choice is an open question (WeasyPrint sidecar vs doc-service vs headless Chrome).

title: Stale files/config in legacy repo (post-cutover cleanup only)
description: NO CHANGES while legacy is live. Catalogue: .flaskenv points at non-existent app.py (real entry run.py); debug=True hardcoded in run.py; FLASK_SECRET_KEY defined in .env but never read; empty 0-byte file named 'docker'; 'docker-compose.dev old.yml' (references lowercase dockerfileforpi — case-sensitivity trap); Dockerfile-old; templates/obsolete/ (4 dead templates); utils/project_filter.py queries non-existent 'projects_register' table and is never imported; utils/max_rev.py unused; manual delivery address code paths disabled but present.

title: Broad except-Exception swallowing hides errors
description: _to_date, _to_float, several fetchers, and _acquire_singleflight (fails OPEN — a lock error allows duplicate Outlook drafts) all swallow exceptions. New app: explicit error handling, surface failures, fail closed on the email single-flight.

title: Hardcoded VAT 20% and GBP currency
description: VAT computed as net_total * 0.2 in three places (routes.py:266,905,1021); currency hardcoded 'GBP' in utils/forms.py. Centralise both in config/constants in the new app (single source, still matching legacy output during parity testing).

title: Dead/duplicated code in supabase_client.py — do not port
description: supabase_client.py (~1075 lines) defines fetch_suppliers 3x (lines 132, 194, 402), fetch_projects 2x (153, 283), fetch_projects_map 2x (165, 375) — only the last definition wins. A second get_next_revision at line 912 collides with utils/revision.py's; routes use the utils one. Also unused-but-installed deps: flask_sqlalchemy (configured against sqlite that is never created), psycopg2-binary, supabase-py. Port only the live code paths.
